channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Macron's diplomatic comeback: from France's domestic crisis to reshaping Europe's defense
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron is back at the center of global diplomacy, seeking to ease relations with President Donald Trump, championing a Ukraine peace plan alongside his British counterpart, and seeing his longstanding desire to boost European defense turning into reality.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China learned from Trump's first trade war and changed its tactics when tariffs came again
item.description: The leaders of both Canada and Mexico got on the phone with President Donald Trump this past week to seek solutions after he slapped tariffs on their countries, but China’s president appears unlikely to make a similar call soon.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh brace for upcoming food reductions as aid agencies cut funding
item.description: COX’S BAZAR, Bangladesh (AP) — Rohingya refugees in crammed Bangladeshi camps say they are worried about a U.S.
item.dc:creator: SHAFIQUR RAHMAN and JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran's supreme leader rejects nuclear talks with US after Trump's overtures
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader, said in a series of X posts Saturday he would not negotiate a nuclear deal with the U.S. due to its demands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iranian leader rejects Trump’s push for nuclear talks
item.description: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei rejected President Trump’s push for nuclear deal talks between the two nations, arguing the initiative is only a gateway for Washington to impose new demands and limit Tehran’s military capabilities. “Some coercive governments insist on negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 12 people injured by 3 men shooting randomly at Toronto pub customers
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A dozen people were injured in a shooting at an eastern Toronto pub in what police called a reckless act of violence by three men who entered the bar and fired randomly without warning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill more than 20 as Polish prime minister warns against appeasement
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched heavy aerial attacks on Ukraine for a second night Saturday after the United States stopped sharing satellite images with the Ukrainian government, officials said. At least 22 people have been killed. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard's warning to Senate on Syria proves prophetic as Al Qaeda-linked regime slaughters minorities
item.description: Amid efforts by the U.N and Europe to woo former terror chief Ahmed al-Sharaa with sanctions relief, his regime is accused of killing hundreds of civilians from the minority Alawite community.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon released from prison
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday, a day after a Seoul court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial for rebellion without being detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gaza plan criticized by US and Israel gets European support
item.description: Foreign ministers from France, Germany, Italy and the UK announced their support for Egypt's Gaza reconstruction plan, despite Israel and the US expressing reservations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tropical low tracks west across Australian east coast leaving 1 dead and several injured
item.description: One person has died and several were injured as flooding rains lash the Australian east coast even though it avoided its first tropical cyclone in 51 years.
item.dc:creator: John Pye And Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol freed from prison after canceled arrest
item.description: South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday after a court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Polish government plans mandatory military training for adult men
item.description: The Polish government is working on a plan to prepare large-scale military training for all of its adult men, according to Prime Minister Donald Tusk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clashes in Syria between government forces and Assad loyalists kill more than 200
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Fighters siding with Syria's new government stormed several villages near the country's coast, killing dozens of men in response to recent attacks on government security forces by loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad, a war monitor said.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 90,000 Palestinians attend the first Friday prayers of Ramadan in Jerusalem
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — In the first Friday prayers of the Muslim holy month of Ramadan, about 90,000 Palestinians prayed at the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound in Jerusalem’s Old City under tight security by Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: IMAD ISSEID and JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: In 2015, Obama committed the US to achieving UN global goals by 2030. Trump just rejected the goals
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — In 2015, then-President Barack Obama committed the United States to achieving newly adopted U.N. global goals by 2030, including ending poverty, achieving gender equality and urgently tackling climate change.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Far-right front-runner in Romania’s annulled presidential race files his candidacy in the rerun
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanian far-right populist Calin Georgescu filed his candidacy Friday in a rerun of last year's presidential election that was annulled by a top court after his surprise first-round win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens new sanctions, tariffs on Russia amid push for ceasefire in Ukraine
item.description: President Trump on Friday said he was weighing additional sanctions and tariffs on Russia in a push to bring Moscow to the negotiating table to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: UN prepping for spending cuts as DOGE roots out waste in US, internal docs show
item.description: Fox News Digital obtained an internal United Nations memo indicating that the international body is preparing heads of departments for funding cuts as DOGE roots out waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unexploded WWII bomb in Paris halts Eurostar travel to London, Brussels
item.description: Eurostar announced the cancellation of all its high-speed trains connecting Paris to London and Brussels on Friday after the discovery of a World War-II era bomb near the tracks serving Eurostar’s hub in the French capital, Gare du Nord.
item.dc:creator: SAMUEL PETREQUIN and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China slams Trump-imposed ‘arbitrary tariffs,’ vows retaliation against US
item.description: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on Friday accused the United States of "meeting good with evil" for imposing a new round of "arbitrary tariffs" and vowed retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mother of Abbey Gate victim says Biden ‘robbed’ family of justice
item.description: "If we can take one terrorist off the list of harming Americans, especially our soldiers, then that is a good day," Paula Knauss Selph told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: James Harrison, whose blood saved 2.4M babies dies, at 88
item.description: An Australian man credited with saving 2.4 million babies through his record-breaking blood plasma donations over six decades, has died at 88, his family said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis suffered two new episodes of acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept a new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel stopped the entry of all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” if Hamas doesn’t accept a new proposal to extend a fragile ceasefire, while key mediator Egypt accused Israel of using “starvation as a weapon.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as European leaders assembled in London to discuss ending the war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
